english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

Tom counts and categorizes birds that land in the tree in his backyard every evening from 10:00-11:00 pm and from 2:00-3:00 am. He counts mostly owls and generalizes, “most birds that land in my tree throughout the night are owls.”
ugf_translation: The kind of wrong idea is a quick guess from a small group. The person who watches and puts birds into groups that land on the tree in his yard each night during two short times. He sees mostly night birds that catch food at night and says, “most birds that land in my tree all night are night birds that catch food.”

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

A poll administered to 10,000 registered voters who were home-owners showed that 90% supported a policy to slash Medicaid funding and decrease property taxes. Therefore, 90% of voters support a policy to slash Medicaid funding.
ugf_translation: Find what kind of bad thing, if any, the number talk about many things has. An ask asked to 10,000 people who own houses showed that 9 out of 10 said they liked a plan to cut money for body help and lower house money. So, 9 out of 10 people like a plan to cut money for body help.

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

A telephone poll administered by a computer randomly generating numbers to call, found that 68% of Americans in the sample of 2000 were in favor of legalizing recreational marijuana use. Thus, almost 70% of Americans favor legalizing recreation marijuana use.
ugf_translation:
Find what kind of trouble, if any, the number picture has.
A computer that calls people by picking numbers by chance asked a lot of people. It found that about 68 out of 100 people in the group of 2000 said they like the idea of letting people use the green thing that grows from the ground that makes you feel funny for fun. So, almost 70 out of 100 people like the idea of letting people use that green thing for fun.

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

A randomized telephone poll in the United States asked respondents whether they supported a) a policy that allows killing innocent children in the womb or b) a policy that saves the lives of innocent children in the womb. The results showed that 69% of respondents choose option “b” over option “a.” The generalization was made that “most Americans favor a policy that disallows abortion.”
ugf_translation: Find what kind of trouble, if any, the number story has. A by chance phone ask in the big country of many people asked people if they liked the big idea that lets the ending of tiny people inside a mother or the big idea that keeps the tiny people inside a mother alive. The answer numbers showed that about seven out of ten people chose the second idea instead of the first. The big idea said that most people in the big country like the big idea that does not let the ending of tiny people inside a mother.

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

Steve’s first rock and roll concert was an Ani Difranco concert, in which most of the concert-goers were women with feminist political slogans written on their t-shirts. Steve makes the generalization that “most rock and roll concert-goers are women who are feminists.” He then applies this generalization to the next concert he attends (Tom Petty) and is greatly surprised by what he finds.
ugf_translation:
Find what kind of trouble, if any, the number idea has.

The first loud music show with thin long thing that makes sound that the man went to was a show by the person who makes music with voice and talks about women and fair. Most of the people at that show were women who wore shirts with words about women being the same. The man said the idea that "most people at loud music shows are women who want women to be the same." He then used this idea at the next loud music show he went to (the man who makes music with voice about cars and roads) and was very surprised by what he saw.

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

A high school principal conducts a survey of how satisfied students are with his high school by asking students in detention to fill out a satisfaction survey. Generalizing from that sample, he infers that 79% of students are dissatisfied with their high school experience. He is surprised and saddened by the result. 147
ugf_translation:
Find what kind of wrong idea, if any, the number guess has.

A school leader asks the kids who are in time out to write down how they feel about the school. He then says that about 79 out of 100 kids do not like the school. He feels surprise and sad about the end thing.

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

After having attended numerous Pistons home games over 20 years, Alice cannot remember a time when she didn’t see ticket scalpers selling tickets outside the stadium. She generalizes that there are always scalpers at every Pistons home game.
ugf_translation:
Find what kind of wrong, if any, the idea from numbers has.

After going to many the team that plays with a round ball home games for 20 years, the person who went to the games cannot remember a time when she didn’t see paper that lets you go inside sellers who sell paper that lets you go inside not allowed outside the big place where the game is. She says that there are always paper that lets you go inside sellers who sell paper that lets you go inside not allowed at every the team that plays with a round ball home game.

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

After having attended numerous Pistons home games over 20 years, Alice cannot remember a time when she didn’t see ticket scalpers selling tickets outside the stadium. She generalizes that there are ticket scalpers at every NBA game.
ugf_translation: Say what kind of problem, if any, the number made from many things idea has. After going to many games of the big city ball game with two groups for 20 years, the person cannot think of a time when she did not see people who sell paper that lets you go inside outside the building. She thinks that there are people who sell paper that lets you go inside at every game with the big ball that two groups play.

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

After having attended numerous Pistons home games over 20 years, Alice cannot remember a time when she didn’t see ticket scalpers selling tickets outside the stadium. She generalizes that there are ticket scalpers at every sporting event.
ugf_translation:
Find what kind of trouble, if any, the number guess has.

After going to many of the big ball games for a long time, the person cannot think of a time when she did not see people who sell paper that lets you go inside outside the big building where people watch games. She makes a quick guess that there are people who sell paper that lets you go inside at every team game.

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

Bob once ordered a hamburger from Burger King and got violently ill shortly after he ate it. From now on, he never eats at Burger King because he fears he will get food poisoning.
ugf_translation: Point out what kind of problem, if any, the idea that a small set of numbers can tell about a big group has. The man once asked for the round red food that comes from animals on a soft baked food from the fast food place that sells that food and got very sick right after he ate it. From now on, he does not eat at that fast food place because he is scared he will get bad food making him sick.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

Every painting by Rembrandt contains dark colors and illuminated faces, therefore the original painting that hangs in my high school is probably by Rembrandt, since it contains dark colors and illuminated faces.
ugf_translation: The reason is not strong. The well known old person who makes pictures of the past makes pictures that have dark colors and bright faces. The first picture that hangs on the wall in my big school for older kids has dark colors and bright faces. Because the picture has dark colors and bright faces, the reason says it is probably made by that well known old person who makes pictures of the past.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

I was once bitten by a poodle. Therefore, this poodle will probably bite me too.
ugf_translation: it is big. I got a mark from the small dog with hair that go round. so this small dog will maybe mark me again.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

Every poodle I’ve ever met has bitten me (and I’ve met over 300 poodles). Therefore, this poodle will probably bite me too.
ugf_translation: look at the idea that says one thing is like another and say if it is strong or not strong. all of the small dogs with hair that is bent I have ever met have got a bite from me and I have met more than 300 of them. so this small dog with hair that is bent will maybe get a bite from me too.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

My friend took Dr. Van Cleave’s logic class last semester and got an A. Since Dr. Van Cleave’s class is essentially the same this semester and since my friend is no better a student than I am, I will probably get an A as well.
ugf_translation: look at the kind of reason that says because two things are like each other they will have the same thing that happens as strong or not strong. my friend took the person who shows how to think about right and wrong thinking class last time and got a good high sign. because that person who shows how to think about right and wrong class is almost the same this time and because my friend is not a better person who learns than I am, I will probably get a good high sign too.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

Bill Cosby used his power and position to seduce and rape women. Therefore, Bill Cosby probably also used his power to rob banks.
ugf_translation: look if the talk is strong or not strong. the well known TV man used his power and position to make a woman want to have sex and to force a woman to have sex. so the well known TV man probably also used his power to take money from a bank.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

Every car I’ve ever owned had seats, wheels and brakes and was also safe to drive. This used car that I am contemplating buying has seats, wheels and brakes. Therefore, this used car is probably safe to drive.
ugf_translation: look at the like idea and say if it is good or not good. all the moving boxes I have had had places to sit, round things that turn, and things that stop, and they were also safe to move. this old moving box I think about getting has places to sit, round things that turn, and things that stop. so this old moving box is probably safe to move.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

Every Volvo I’ve ever owned was a safe car to drive. My new car is a Volvo. Therefore, my new car is probably safe to drive.
ugf_translation: look at the like idea as strong or not strong. all the cars that I have owned that are the big metal box with four wheels from the place that makes safe cars were safe to drive. my new big metal box with four wheels is from that same place. so my new big metal box with four wheels is maybe safe to drive.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

Dr. Van Cleave did not give Jones an excused absence when Jones missed class for his grandmother’s funeral. Mary will have to miss class to attend her aunt’s funeral. Therefore, Dr. Van Cleave should not give Mary an excused absence either.
ugf_translation: look at the like idea and say if it is not strong or strong. a doctor did not give the first student a not go when the first student did not go to class because he went to the time when a person who died is remembered for his older woman family member. the second student will have to not go to class because she will go to the time when a person who died is remembered for her woman aunt. so the doctor should not give the second student a not go either.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

Dr. Van Cleave did not give Jones an excused absence when Jones missed class for his brother’s birthday party. Mary will have to miss class to attend her aunt’s funeral. Therefore, Dr. Van Cleave should not give Mary an excused absence either. 159
ugf_translation: Look at the idea as strong or not strong. The teacher did not give the student an allowed not to be there when the student missed class for his brother the day we have a party for being born. The woman student will have to miss class to go to the day we say the dead person is not here of her aunt. So the teacher should not give the woman student an allowed not to be there either.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

If health insurance companies pay for heart surgery and brain surgery, which can both increase an individual’s happiness, then they should also pay for cosmetic surgery, which can also increase an individual’s happiness.
ugf_translation: Look at if the way of seeing how two things are the same idea is good or not good. If the group that give money for sick heart fix and sick brain fix, which can both make a person more happy, then they should also give money for look change fix, which can also make a person more happy.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

A knife is an eating utensil that can cut things. A spoon is also an eating utensil. So a spoon can probably cut things as well.
ugf_translation: Look at the same talk and say if it is not strong or strong. The sharp thing for cutting is a thing for eating that can cut stuff. The round thing for eating is also a thing for eating. So the round thing for eating can maybe cut stuff too.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

Any artificial, complex object like a watch or a telescope has been designed by some intelligent human designer. But naturally occurring objects like eyes and brains are also very complex objects. Therefore, complex naturally occurring objects must have been designed by some intelligent non-human designer.
ugf_translation: Say if the like idea is strong or not strong. All made hard things like a hand time thing or a far seeing long round thing were made by a thinking human person. But things that happen in the big place that is not made by people like eyes and brains are also hard things. So hard things that happen in the big place that is not made by people must have been made by a thinking not human being.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

The world record holding runner, Kenenisa Bekele ran 100 miles per week and twice a week did workouts comprised of ten mile repeats on the track in the weeks leading up to his 10,000 meter world record. I have run 100 miles per week and have been doing ten mile repeats twice a week. Therefore, the next race I will run will probably be a world record.
ugf_translation:
Look at the idea that is like the way of look at two things to see how they are alike and say if it is strong or not strong.

The person who has the fastest time in the whole world for run, the long distance runner from the far away land, used to run about one hundred of the thing people use to count distance like a step each week and two times each week did the work that you do to get better where he ran ten of the thing people use to count distance like a step over and over on the flat circle path before he made the fastest time for ten many hundreds of the small thing people use to count distance like a step.
I also run about one hundred of the thing people use to count distance like a step each week and I also do the work that you do to get better two times each week.
So the next fast run that people watch I run will maybe be the fastest time in the whole world.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

I feel pain when someone hits me in the face with a hockey puck. We are both human beings, so you also probably feel pain when you are hit in the face with a hockey puck.
ugf_translation: Think about the way of putting two ideas side by side idea as big or small. I feel hurt when a person hits me in the front of my head with a round ice thing used in a game. We are both people, so you also maybe feel hurt when you are hit in the front of your head with a round ice thing used in a game.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

The color I experience when I see something as “green” has a particular quality (that is difficult to describe). You and I are both human beings, so the color you experience when you see something green probably has the exact same quality. (That is, what you and I experience when we see something green is the exact same experiential color.)
ugf_translation: Look at the talk that uses one thing to think about another thing and pick if it is strong or not strong. The color I feel when I see a thing that is called green has a special feeling that is hard to put into words. You and I are both human bodies, so the color you feel when you see a green thing probably has the same feeling. That means what you and I feel when we see a green thing is the same feeling of color.

english_source:
For each correlation, identify whether A causes B, B causes A, a common cause is at work, or the correlation is accidental.

There is a positive correlation between U.S. spending on science, space, and technology (A) and suicides by hanging, strangulation, and suffocation (B).
ugf_translation: For each when two things go up or down together, find if A makes the group of people who end their own life by pulling themselves up with the long thing made of many thin strings, by stopping the air, or by stopping getting air happen, the group makes A happen, something makes both happen, or the two go together by chance. There is a when two things go up or down together that is up between the money that the big country spends on learning about the world, the sky, and machines (A) and the group of people who end their own life by pulling themselves up with the long thing made of many thin strings, by stopping the air, or by stopping getting air.

english_source:
For each correlation, identify whether A causes B, B causes A, a common cause is at work, or the correlation is accidental.

There is a positive correlation between our dog Charlie’s weight (A) and the amount of time we spend away from home (B). That is, the more time we spend away from home, the heavier Charlie gets (and the more we are at home, the lighter Charlie is.
ugf_translation: for each tie say if one thing makes the other or the other makes the one or a shared thing makes both or it is just chance. there is a up tie between our dog the long animal with sharp teeth that lives in a house weight and the many of time we are not in the house. that means the more time we are not in the house the more heavy the dog gets and the more we are in the house the less heavy the dog is.

english_source:
For each correlation, identify whether A causes B, B causes A, a common cause is at work, or the correlation is accidental.

The height of the tree in our front yard (A) positively correlates with the height of the shrub in our backyard (B).
ugf_translation: For each pair that moves together, find if A makes the second thing, the second thing makes A, the same cause is at work, or the pair is by chance. The tall size of the tree in our front yard (A) goes up with the tall size of the small green thing with many leaf in our back yard (the second thing).

english_source:
For each correlation, identify whether A causes B, B causes A, a common cause is at work, or the correlation is accidental.

There is a negative correlation between the number of suicide bombings in the U.S. (A) and the number of hairs on a particular U.S President’s head (B).
ugf_translation: For each pair, say if the first thing makes the second thing, the second thing makes the first thing, both are made by the same other thing, or the pair just happened by chance. There is a bad pair between how many own hurt hurts happen in the country of the free and the number of hairs on the head of one leader of that country.

english_source:
For each correlation, identify whether A causes B, B causes A, a common cause is at work, or the correlation is accidental.

There is a high positive correlation between the number of fire engines in a particular borough of New York City (A) and the number of fires that occur there (B). Case 2 ~A ~B C D ~G Case 3 A ~B C ~D ~G Case 1 A B ~C D ~G Case 2 ~A ~B ~C D ~G Case 3 A ~B ~C ~D ~G Case 1 A B C D G Case 2 ~A ~B C D G Case 3 A ~B ~C D ~G Case 1 ~A B ~C D ~G Case 2 ~A B C D G Case 3 ~A ~B ~C D G 170
ugf_translation: for each pair look to see if one thing makes the other thing, the other thing makes the first thing, a same thing makes both things, or the pair is just by chance. there is a strong up pair between the count of big red water trucks in a part of the big city and the count of hot burns that happen there.

english_source:
For each correlation, identify whether A causes B, B causes A, a common cause is at work, or the correlation is accidental.

At one point in history, there was a negative correlation between the number of mules in the state (A) and the salaries paid to professors at the state university (B). That is, the more mules, the lower the professors’ salaries.
ugf_translation: For each pair, say if A makes the second thing, the second thing makes A, a same thing makes both, or the pair is just by chance. A long time ago, when there were more long animals that carry big things in the state, the money for teachers at the state school was less. In other words, more of those animals meant less money for teachers.

english_source:
For each correlation, identify whether A causes B, B causes A, a common cause is at work, or the correlation is accidental.

There is a strong positive correlation between the number of traffic accidents on a particular highway (A) and the number of billboards featuring scantily-clad models (B).
ugf_translation: For each pair, say if the first thing makes the second thing, the second thing makes the first thing, a same thing makes both, or the pair is by chance. There is a big good pair between the count of car hits on one road (the first thing) and the count of big signs with almost not much clothing people (the second thing).

english_source:
For each correlation, identify whether A causes B, B causes A, a common cause is at work, or the correlation is accidental.

The girth of an adult’s waist (A) is negatively correlated with the height of their vertical leap (B).
ugf_translation: for each connection, find if A makes the second thing, the second thing makes A, a same cause is there, or the connection is by chance. the round size of a grown up middle (A) goes down when the how high they can jump straight up the second thing goes up.

english_source:
For each correlation, identify whether A causes B, B causes A, a common cause is at work, or the correlation is accidental.

Olympic marathon times (A) are positively correlated with the temperature during the marathon (B). That is, the more time it takes an Olympic marathoner to complete the race, the higher the temperature.
ugf_translation: For each connection, find if the time makes the heat, the heat makes the time, the same reason makes both, or the connection is by chance. The time it takes a person who runs in the big world long foot game goes up when the heat during the foot game goes up. That means the longer the person who runs needs to finish, the hotter it is.

english_source:
For each correlation, identify whether A causes B, B causes A, a common cause is at work, or the correlation is accidental.

The number gray hairs on an individual’s head (A) is positively correlated with the number of children or grandchildren they have (B).
ugf_translation: for each pair find if A makes the other thing, the other thing makes A, the same thing makes both, or the pair is by chance. the number of gray hair on a human head (A) goes up together with the number of that human children or children of a child (the other thing).
